items.summary: Manchester City have reached an agreement with Nottingham Forest to sign Elliot Anderson, the club have confirmed.

items.title: 'Nervous' Sciver-Brunt revels as 'close to complete' England reach home final
items.summary: Nat Sciver-Brunt was delighted to be able to overcome pre-match nerves and guide England to the Women’s T20 World Cup final with a ‘close to complete’ 40-run victory over South Africa.

items.title: Cristiano Ronaldo, 41, makes history in Portugal’s World Cup knockout clash against Croatia
items.summary: CRISTIANO RONALDO became the World Cup’s oldest knock-out player ever by starting against Croatia aged 41 years and 147 days. items.title: Fernández exploring Chelsea exit options - agent
items.summary: Enzo Fernández's agent has confirmed the midfielder is exploring options to leave Chelsea this summer.

items.title: No reds for mouth covering in Champions League
items.summary: Players will not be sent off for covering their mouths when speaking to an opponent in UEFA competitions, the governing body confirmed.

items.title: Oyarzabal fires Spain past Austria to WC last 16
items.summary: Mikel Oyarzabal scored twice as Spain cruised into the World Cup round of 16 with a 3-0 win over Austria at SoFi Stadium on Thursday.

items.title: Tielemans on Trossard fight: 'Part of football'
items.summary: Youri Tielemans has played down talks of a rift with teammate Leandro Trossard following their on-field scuffle during Belgium's round-of-32 World Cup win against Senegal.

items.title: Kerr says NWSL only choice after Chelsea exit
items.summary: Gotham FC forward Sam Kerr said that she only considered returning to the NWSL, where she is still the league's leader in regular season goals, when she decided to leave Chelsea after six years at the London club.

items.title: After Spain win, Yamal says 'World Cup starts now'...
items.summary: Lamine Yamal said "the World Cup starts now" after shining in Spain's 3-0 win over Austria at SoFi Stadium, with the Barcelona star being named the game's MVP.

items.title: Spain finally have World Cup mojo back with first ...
items.summary: Heading into the World Cup, Spain's record since their 2010 triumph was three tournaments, zero knockout wins. But after beating Austria 3-0, they're back in business.
